## Page 36

### [p036_c0001] Statutory Provisions for Service Change and Fare & Toll Changes
[p036_c0002] Under the provisions of Title 49, Chapter 53, Section 5307, of the United States Code, the MTA, as a recipient of federal capital and operating assistance funding, must have a “locally developed process” to solicit and consider public comment before a major service change and/or fare and toll changes.
[p036_c0003] Pursuant to this federal requirement, a public hearing is required before the implementation of a bus, subway, or commuter railroad service change if the proposed change involves any of the following conditions:
> [p036_c0004] The diagram consists of three vertical panels. The left panel, titled 'Minor Service Change*', lists three criteria: 'Less than 25% of the frequency of service and,', 'Less than 25% of the route mileage and,', and 'Involving a reduction of less than one operating hour a day.' The middle panel, titled 'Minor and Major Service Change Guidelines', contains a blue arrow pointing left, a blue arrow pointing right, and two text blocks: '*Must meet all three “Minor” criteria.' and '**Must meet any one of the criteria on the list.' The right panel, titled 'Major Service Change**', lists three criteria: '25% or more of a route's frequency or route mileage, or', 'involving a reduction in operating hours that exceed one hour a day,', and 'or the partial or complete closing of a subway or commuter railroad station.' A credit '(Credit: MTA-NYCT Rail Planning)' is at the bottom left. Minor Service Change* (Aggregated over a 12-month period) are defined as those resulting in: Less than 25% of the frequency of service and, Less than 25% of the route mileage and, Involving a reduction of less than one operating hour a day. (Credit: MTA-NYCT Rail Planning) Minor and Major Service Change Guidelines The service change guidelines specify criteria for major and minor changes. Major Service Change** (Aggregated over a 12-month period) are defined as those resulting in: 25% or more of a route's frequency or route mileage, or involving a reduction in operating hours that exceed one hour a day, or the partial or complete closing of a subway or commuter railroad station. ** Must meet ANY one of the criteria.
[p036_c0005] Note: Public Hearings are not required for service changes under the following:
- [p036_c0006] • Demonstration projects or experimental service. • Standard seasonal or holiday adjustments and special event services. • Temporary changes due to emergency situations, service disruptions, or events beyond the control of the MTA.
### [p036_c0007] Public Hearings for Service Changes/Fare and Toll Changes
[p036_c0008] A public hearing is required for any proposed service change that is generally defined as noted above. The MTA Board has approved definitions of “major” service change for each agency and incorporated them into the Federal Transit Administration’s “locally developed process”.
[p036_c0009] _32_
[p036_c0010] _Master Page # 36 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 37

### [p037_c0001] Facilitating Public Participation in Public Hearings
[p037_c0002] During public hearings a clear structure is established, starting with an opening statement with an introduction of topic and MTA officials present, followed by presentations and designated period for public comment. Facilitators ensure that all participants have equal opportunities to speak, often implementing time speaking slots to accommodate a high volume of attendees. The MTA encourages respectful exchanges and provides multiple ways for individuals to participate, including in-person comments, written submissions, and virtual participation options. These facilitation efforts create a welcoming environment that supports productive and meaningful engagement.
### [p037_c0003] 5.6 Collecting Public Comments
[p037_c0004] Public comments are a valuable source of feedback for the MTA, informing decisions and shaping policy. During and after public hearings and meetings, the MTA collects comments in various forms, to ensure accessibility and inclusiveness.
### [p037_c0005] 1. Comment Collection Methods
- [p037_c0006] • In-Person Comments: Spoken comments during public hearings or meetings limited to two minutes. • Written Comments: Submitted via forms at hearings/meetings, mailed letters, or online submissions through the MTA hearing portal. • Email and Telephone: Specialized email addresses and public hearing hotlines for collecting public input, including those tailored for specific projects. • Virtual Meetings: Chat features or designated time for verbal comments during online sessions. • Translated Comments: Although not frequent, comments are accepted in multiple languages in compliance with people who speak English less than very well.
### [p037_c0007] 2. Recordkeeping
[p037_c0008] All comments, regardless of format, are recorded for analysis.
[p037_c0009] Note Taking: An informal record to prepare the board staff summary and draft consideration of substantive comments. The official transcript of the hearing/meeting is the document of record that would be used to inform the Board of substantive comments.
[p037_c0010] _33_
[p037_c0011] _Master Page # 37 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 38

- [p038_c0001] • Transcription: Spoken comments are transcribed or summarized. The official transcript of the hearing/meeting is the document of record that would be used of substantive comments. • Digital Recording: Meetings are often recorded (audio or video) to ensure accurate documentation.
### [p038_c0002] Compliance
[p038_c0003] The MTA follows local, state, and federal guidelines, including Title VI, to ensure fairness in the comment collection process.
> [p038_c0004] A large, solid blue arrow pointing to the right, positioned horizontally across the page. It has a slight 3D effect with a darker blue shadow on the right side.
### [p038_c0005] 6. Public Participation Methods
### [p038_c0006] 6.1 Surveys
[p038_c0007] The MTA utilizes attendee surveys to understand which members of the public are attending public hearings, as well as a key tool to gather information and data about community opinions, preferences, and needs. These attendee surveys allow the MTA to capture and provide valuable insights into riders' experiences, and priorities.
[p038_c0008] The MTA uses both online and in-person surveys, with formats tailored to gather feedback on specific issues, such as accessibility and proposed projects. Participation is not mandatory or required for hearing attendees.
[p038_c0009] Attendee surveys are distributed through multiple channels to maximize reach, including providing both a scannable QR code, and a link for the online version of the survey and on-site paper distribution. For these attendee surveys, the MTA uses user friendly platforms that ensure ease of access, and mobile compatibility. Links to online surveys are also included in the confirmation email of all registrants with the logistics for the public hearing. When on-site, physical copies of the attendee survey are available near the registration table with MTA staff available to assist attendees and ensure all feedback is captured.
[p038_c0010] _34_
[p038_c0011] _Master Page # 38 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 39

[p039_c0001] The attendee surveys are available in multiple languages. The combined multi agency list of languages was determined by the Title VI team across all agencies. The Agency-wide top 9 non-English languages include: Arabic, Bengali, Chinese, Haitian Creole, Italian, Korean, Russian, Spanish, and Yiddish. This ensures that people who speak English less than very well have equal access to participate.
[p039_c0002] * Portuguese and Polish are specific Metro-North and Long Island Railroad Languages.
### [p039_c0003] 6.2 Online Forums and Webinars
[p039_c0004] In recognition of the growing need for accessible and flexible participation options, the MTA hosts online forums and virtual meetings, allowing individuals to engage from the comfort of their homes or workplace. These virtual options have expanded the MTA's ability to reach individuals who may be unable to attend in-person meetings/events due to scheduling conflicts or mobility issues.
### [p039_c0005] • Online Forums
[p039_c0006] Online forums are digital spaces where participants can discuss specific topics, provide feedback, and ask questions over an extended period. Forums allow participants to join conversations at their convenience, making it easy for individuals with varying schedules to contribute. The MTA moderates these forums to ensure a respectful and constructive environment with representatives answering questions, clarifying information, and encouraging dialogue on key issues. Forums also allow community members to interact with each other, share perspectives, and build consensuses on topics of shared interest.
### [p039_c0007] • Virtual Meetings/Webinars
[p039_c0008] Webinars are live, online events where MTA officials present information and interact directly with the public in real-time. These meetings are held through video conferencing platforms, with options for video, audio, and text-based participation. Webinars typically follow a structured agenda, including a presentation, updates on ongoing projects, and a designated time for public comments or questions. These meetings are often recorded and made available on the MTA website, allowing individuals who could not attend to view the content later. Questions are submitted in advance and via the Q&A chat function.
[p039_c0009] Accessibility and Language Assistance Services:
[p039_c0010] ASL and CART Captioning are provided.
[p039_c0011] _35_
[p039_c0012] _Master Page # 39 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 40

### [p040_c0001] 7. Title VI Compliance and Community Engagement
### [p040_c0002] Engagement with The Following Populations
### [p040_c0003] 7.1 Low-Income and Minority Communities
[p040_c0004] The MTA recognizes the unique challenges faced by low-income and minority communities, who often rely on public transit to access employment, healthcare, education and other essential services. To engage these communities effectively, the MTA conducts targeted outreach to ensure all voices are represented in decisions impacting transit access, affordability, and service quality. The MTA offers fare reduction programs, like the Fare Fairs initiative, to support affordability for low-income riders. Outreach campaigns inform eligible individuals about these programs, along with clear instructions on how to apply. The MTA also collects feedback from these communities through surveys and dedicated sessions, ensuring that their unique perspectives are considered in service planning and policy changes. By implementing these engagement strategies, the MTA aims to address transportation access and create a transit system that better serves all communities.
### [p040_c0005] 7.2 People with Disabilities
[p040_c0006] To engage people with disabilities, the MTA collaborates closely with individuals with disabilities and disability advocacy groups, incorporating their input into service planning and accessibility improvements. Regular discussions with these organizations/advisory committees ensure that the unique needs of people with disabilities are front and center in MTA's decision-making processes. For example, the MTA's Access-A-Ride service provides door-to-door transportation for individuals with disabilities who cannot use the standard transit system. Public feedback on Access-A-Ride and other accessible services is continuously gathered to inform service enhancements. The Advisory Committee for Transit Accessibility (ACTA) is a group of community members committed to working with the MTA on a range of accessibility issues that collects community and rider feedback. This includes feedback on subways, buses, and Access-A-Ride paratransit. ACTA also helps the MTA develop new accessibility initiatives and promotes these projects to the community.
[p040_c0007] For public meetings and events, the MTA ensures compliance with ADA requirements, offering accessible venues, sign language interpreters, assistive listening devices, and materials in Braille and large print upon advance request. Virtual meetings are also designed to be accessible, featuring captioning, screen reader-compatible platforms, and options for audio-only participation. The MTA
[p040_c0008] _36_
[p040_c0009] _Master Page # 40 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 41

[p041_c0001] actively seeks feedback from people with disabilities on infrastructure projects, such as elevator installations and station accessibility improvements, to make sure upgrades align with actual community needs.
### [p041_c0002] 7.3 People Who Speak English Less Than Very Well
[p041_c0003] In New York's multilingual community, the MTA strives to make transit information and engagement opportunities accessible to people who speak English less than very well. Recognizing that language barriers can limit access to essential services and reduce participation, the MTA offers a robust language access program to ensure that non-English speakers have the information they need to use public transit effectively and contribute their perspectives on transit issues.
[p041_c0004] The MTA translates key documents including service updates, legal notices, surveys, and fare information into the Agency-wide top 9 non-English languages such as: Arabic, Bengali, Chinese, Haitian Creole, Italian, Korean, Russian, Spanish, and Yiddish. [Some additional translation includes Polish and Portuguese]. Additionally, bilingual staff and language interpreting services are available at public events, hearings, and meetings, ensuring that people who speak English less than very well can engage fully in the discussion. Virtual meetings also offer multilingual support, with interpretation options available upon request.
### [p041_c0005] 7.4 Seniors
[p041_c0006] Seniors are a vital part of the MTA's rider base and often rely on public transit for their daily transportation needs. The MTA recognizes that older adults may face specific challenges, such as reduced mobility, visual and/or hearing disabilities, technological barriers, safety concerns, and takes the necessary steps to address these needs through targeted engagement and service strategies.
[p041_c0007] The MTA offers an equitable payment solution for more than a million passengers who receive fare discounts. The Reduced-Fare OMNY program provides discounted fares for seniors, making transit more affordable and accessible. The MTA conducted a multi-month soft launch, in which several hundred Reduced-fare customers transitioned from MetroCard to OMNY online and used OMNY on subway and buses. Information about this program was promoted through community centers for
[p041_c0008] older adults, and with MTA representatives and Government & Community Relation staff conducting in-person outreach to explain eligibility requirements, provide language interpretation services, and
[p041_c0009] _37_
[p041_c0010] _Master Page # 41 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 42

[p042_c0001] staff assisted with applications. As of January 2026, Reduced-Fare OMNY is in full use systemwide.
[p042_c0002] At public meetings, the MTA ensures that venues are accessible venues for all ages, offering seating accommodations, accessible entryways, and clear signage. For virtual engagement, age-friendly platforms with straightforward navigation and instructions are chosen to make participation easier for older adults who may be less comfortable with digital tools. Many New Yorkers with disabilities rely on Reduced-Fare when accessing mass transit to get around New York City. By enabling Reduced-Fare customers to make the switch, the MTA is making its transit system quicker and easier for all riders with the tap of their phone or contactless card.
### [p042_c0003] 8. ADA Compliance
### [p042_c0004] Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) Compliance
[p042_c0005] The Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) is a federal civil rights law that prohibits discrimination against people with disabilities in everyday activities. The ADA prohibits discrimination based on disability just as other civil rights laws prohibit discrimination on the basis of race, color, sex, national origin, age, and religion. The ADA guarantees that people with disabilities have the same opportunities as everyone else to enjoy employment opportunities, purchase goods and services, and participate in state and local government programs.
[p042_c0006] People who have vision, hearing, or speech disabilities (“communication disabilities”) use different ways to communicate. For example, people who are blind may give and receive information audibly rather than in writing and people who are deaf may give and receive information through writing or sign language rather than through speech. The MTA is committed to ensuring that all customers can receive information and communicate effectively with the MTA.
[p042_c0007] People who are Blind or have Low Vision may request public hearing information and background material in Braille by contacting the Public Hearing Hotline at 646-252-6777. Representatives are available daily from 6 am to 10 pm. Interested people must allow at least three weeks for the production and delivery of Braille materials.
[p042_c0008] _38_
[p042_c0009] _Master Page # 42 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 43

[p043_c0001] The ADA contains specific requirements for state and local governments to ensure equal access for people with disabilities 5 . In addition, if any person believes that the MTA's efforts to engage the public or involve customers did not comply with the ADA, they have the right to submit a complaint.
### [p043_c0002] 8.1 How to File an ADA Complaint
### [p043_c0003] How to make an ADA-related Complaint or Request
- [p043_c0004] 1. Customer can go to mta.info > Travel > MTA Accessibility > Make an ADA-related Complaint or Request. 2. Complete the online feedback form, or 3. Call 511. 4. All complaints will be entered into and tracked in the MTA's customer database. 5. All customers will receive an initial acknowledgement of their complaint promptly, and where possibly, complaints will be solved on the first contact. 6. Further correspondence and feedback to the customer is determined based on issue type, severity, and customer request(s).
[p043_c0005] The MTA's goal is to resolve all complaints promptly; however, some complaints may require additional investigation and follow-up time.
### [p043_c0006] 8.2 How to Request Services for Language Assistance
[p043_c0007] To request services for language assistance for a MTA public hearing, here are three options:
- [p043_c0008] 1. Online: Check the Accessibility and Language Assistance Services section of the legal notice. The official public hearing notice includes information on how to request accommodation, including accessibility and language assistance services online via the MTA website. 2. Telephone: Dial the Public Hearing Hotline at (646) 252-6777. People who are deaf or hard of hearing can use their preferred relay service or the free 711 relay service, for a communication assistant to facilitate the call to the public hearing hotline. 3. Postal Mail: Write to: MTA Government & Community Relations, Attn: Public Hearing Language Assistance, 2 Broadway, D16.94, New York, NY 10004.
[p043_c0009] _5 Introduction to the American with Disability Act , ADA.gov_
[p043_c0010] _39_
[p043_c0011] _Master Page # 43 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 44

### [p044_c0001] 9. Title VI Compliance
### [p044_c0002] Title VI/Non-Discrimination Policy
[p044_c0003] The Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) and the following affiliated and subsidiary agencies: Long Island Rail Road, Metro-North Railroad, MTA Bus Company, MTA Construction and Development Company, the Grand Central Madison Concourse Operating Company, and New York City Transit Authority, including the Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority and the Staten Island Rapid Transit Operating Authority, and Bridges and Tunnels (herein collectively referred to as “MTA” or “MTA Agencies”) are committed to the tenets of the Title VI Civil Rights Act of 1964, and the assurances of equal access and equitable delivery of transit services, benefits and amenities to customers throughout its service area.
[p044_c0004] Public participation is an integral part of the MTA’s civil rights compliance. The FTA’s Title VI Circular, C4702.1B, requires that MTA develops and implements a public participation plan that includes its outreach efforts to engage minority and limited English proficient populations in its transportation decision-making process.
[p044_c0005] As part of its public participation and outreach, MTA is committed to ensuring that all public participation efforts are inclusive and provide early and continuous opportunities for all to be involved.
### [p044_c0006] 9.1 Filing Title VI and Related Discrimination Complaints
[p044_c0007] If any person believes that they have been discriminated against by the MTA or any MTA Agency based on race, color, national origin, including limited English proficiency, age, sex, disability, or religion, they have the right to file a Title VI Discrimination complaint.
### [p044_c0008] 9.2 How to File a Title VI/Discrimination Complaint
[p044_c0009] Individuals may make a complaint by calling the appropriate Equal Opportunity Division at 800-466-8577, by emailing titlevicomplaints@mtahq.org , or by contacting MTA Customer Service at 511.
[p044_c0010] Individuals may also file a complaint by completing the Title VI and Related Nondiscrimination Laws Complaint Management Form and submitting it in person or mailing it to the appropriate Equal Opportunity Division address listed on the form. Each MTA agency has its own Equal Opportunity
[p044_c0011] _40_
[p044_c0012] _Master Page # 44 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 45

[p045_c0001] Division, and additional information, including links to translated copies of the form available here: Title VI / Non-Discrimination Policy .
[p045_c0002] A complaint is considered filed as of the date it is received by any Customer Service or Equal Opportunity Division. Complaints must be filed within 180 days of the alleged discrimination occurrence. The exception is with regards to sexual harassment allegations, which can be filed up to 3 years after the alleged occurrence, consistent with New York State law.
[p045_c0003] Individuals may also file a complaint directly with U.S. Department of Transportation, Federal Transit Administration, Office of Civil Rights Complaint Team, East Building, 5th Floor—TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p045_c0004] _41_
[p045_c0005] _Master Page # 45 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 46

### [p046_c0001] APPENDICES
[p046_c0002] _42_
[p046_c0003] _Master Page # 46 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 47

### [p047_c0001] ADA Checklist for Events, Meetings & Public Hearings Answer Legend: Yes No N/A = Not Applicable
### [p047_c0002] Venue Accessibility
[p047_c0003]

```text
An accessible route from the subway station to the entrance of the venue. The entrances, exits, restrooms and pathways of the meeting space is ADA-compliant . The venue has a ramp for individuals using wheelchairs or other mobility devices. There is elevator access for individuals using wheelchairs or other mobility devices. The venue has automatic doors (If not, the doorways are at least 32 inches wide and easy to open). There are accessible parking spaces near the entrance of the venue. Tables used for the registration/check-in area are accessible to individuals in wheelchairs/scooters. Discuss Emergency Action Plan preparedness – How to assist individuals with disabilities during an emergency evacuation. Emergency exits are accessible.
```
### [p047_c0004] Meeting Space Setup
[p047_c0005]

```text
[For Open House] There is a clear pathway between tables and chairs. The room is prepped with adequate lighting for people with low vision. Free of strobe or flashing lights. Microphones and speakers are positioned in the room and tested for clear amplification. Podiums: There is an ADA-accessible podium. Theatre style seating is spaced for wheelchair access (36 inches)
```
### [p047_c0006] Language Access / Reasonable Accommodations
[p047_c0007]

```text
ASL Interpreter /CART Captioning /translations services are offered upon advance request. Assistive listening devices are available for individuals who are Hard of Hearing. Multilingual posters and materials are available on-site. If applicable, written materials are translated in a language other than the top 6 LEP languages ( English, Spanish, Russian, Chinese, Haitian-Creole, Korean, and Bengali ). All handouts (written materials) are in accessible digital formats (accessible PDF, plain text or Word doc). Captions and audio descriptions are available for PowerPoint presentations and videos. The images in the PowerPoint Presentation used alternate text and pass the Accessibility Checker. The reading order for table summaries are accessible in the presentation . Ensure all visual aids contain high-contrast colors and large fonts . Refrain from using animations and scripts that make the screen flicker for people with photosensitivity. (Including presentations and digital display boards). Create accessible links: All URLs should be active and properly tagged in the PDF for screen readers.
```

```text
PowerPoint presentation and all digital materials are 508 compliant. Accessibility Checker run before event / high-contrast text and background / Fonts 18 pt (Arial or Calibri). Alt text added for all images/graphics. Descriptive slide text/ Reading order checked. Printed materials available in large print or digital accessible format.
```

## Page 50

### [p050_c0001] DAILY NEWS NYDailyNews.com
### [p050_c0002] Friday, January 3, 2025 13
### [p050_c0003] Public Hearing
### [p050_c0004] CAPITAL PROJECTS PUBLIC HEARING
[p050_c0005] The Metropolitan Transportation Authority, on behalf of MTA New York City Transit and its subsidiaries; MTA Long Island Rail Road; MTA Metro-North Railroad; MTA Construction & Development; and MTA Bus Company (collectively referred to as the "MTA"), will hold a public hearing to solicit comments on MTA's federal grant proposals for Federal Fiscal Year 2025. This hearing will be conducted in a hybrid format, allowing participation both in-person and virtually via Zoom's online platform and conference call feature, and will include a livestream on the MTA 2025 Capital Projects hearing website.
[p050_c0006] The MTA receives federal funds pursuant to the Federal Transit Administration (FTA) of the U.S. Department of Transportation under Sections 5307, 5309, 5310, 5337, 5339 and 5340 of Title 49, United States Code (49 U.S.C. 5307 et seq.), as well as the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (Surface Transportation Act and other federal appropriations). The Governor of the State of New York, local officials, and publicly owned operators of mass transportation services have designated the MTA to be the recipient of these grants.
[p050_c0007] The requested funds are for categories of capital projects listed below and more fully described in the MTA Program of Projects. The MTA's Program of Projects is available for review at https://new.mta.info/transparency/publichearings or by calling the contact number provided below. The MTA solicits and encourages the comments of private transportation providers. Capital improvements under this Program generally take place within the MTA network or on State or city-owned property. Property acquisitions or relocations will be carried out in accordance with relevant laws and regulatory requirements. The projects included in this hearing are part of the approved 2015-2019, 2020-2024 and 2025-2029 Capital Programs.
[p050_c0008] The capital projects have been, or are expected to be, endorsed by the Metropolitan Planning Organizations for the New York metropolitan region and are in compliance with the State Implementation Plan (SIP) as required by the Clean Air Act Amendments of 1990. The projects are not anticipated to have any significant adverse environmental impact. If the FTA prepares a formal environmental impact statement on any of the proposed projects, the MTA will issue a public notice to make the statement available. All legal requirements related to senior citizens and individuals with disabilities will be met.
[p050_c0009] It is anticipated that any difference between the project's cost and the federal grant amount will be covered through funds that are made available by any one or more of the following sources: State, local, affiliated agency funds (such as MTA Bridges and Tunnels or MTA); credits for non-federal project shares generated from toll revenues in accordance with federal statute (23 U.S.C. 120 (i)); or sales of property or program income.
[p050_c0010] In Federal Fiscal Year 2024, MTA received $914.9 million of Section 5307 funds, $1.201 billion of Section 5337 State of Good Repair funds, and $29.8 million of Section 5339 of Bus and Bus Facilities funds. Apportionments from Federal Fiscal Year 2025 are not yet available. Federal funds must generally be matched by a local share contribution for capital assistance of 20 percent for most funding categories.
### [p050_c0011] PROJECT CATEGORIES / ESTIMATED FEDERAL REQUEST (Dollars in Millions)
[p050_c0012]

```text
LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD CONSTRUCTION & DEVELOPMENT Project Categories $M Project Categories $M Stations $184.00 Perm Station Access $2,513.83 Track 244.00 Miscellaneous 40.00 Line Structures 44.00 Total MTA CAD $2,553.83 Communications and Signals 21.00 Shops and Yards 18.00 Power 89.00 Miscellaneous 33.00 Total LIRR $633.00 METRO-NORTH RAIL ROAD NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT Project Categories $M Project Categories $M Stations $8.00 Subway Car $1,800.00 Track and Structures $17.54 Bus Replacement 1,620.00 Communication and Signals 281.50 Passenger Stations 750.00 Power 65.20 Line Equipment 5.00 Shops and Yards 9.60 Line Structures 2,000.00 Miscellaneous 20.80 Signals and Communications 235.00 Ferries 7.40 Power 1,050.00 Total MNR $710.14 Shops and Yards 180.00 Depots 205.00 Service Vehicles 95.00 SIFTOA 142.00 Miscellaneous 100.00 Total NYCT $8,162.00 MTA BUS COMPANY MTA GRAND TOTAL Project Categories $M $12,175.75 Bus Replacement $89.58 Facilities 7.20 Total MTA Bus $96.78
```
### [p050_c0013] DATE AND TIME OF THE PUBLIC HEARING
### [p050_c0014] 2025 CAPITAL PROJECTS PUBLIC HEARING
[p050_c0015] Tuesday, February 4, 2025 - Hearing begins at 6:00 P.M. - Hybrid Public Hearing will include both in person and virtual platforms.
### [p050_c0016] Public Hearing Format
[p050_c0017] This will be a hybrid hearing with in-person participation as well as via Zoom's online platform and conference call feature, with a livestream available on the MTA 2025 Capital Projects hearing website https://new.mta.info/2025-capital-projects-hearing
[p050_c0018] Those interested in speaking must pre-register to speak at the public hearing. Each registered speaker will have two (2) minutes to speak.
### [p050_c0019] Location of the Public Hearing
[p050_c0020] This public hearing will be held in-person at MTA Headquarters, 2 Broadway, 20th Floor – William J. Ronan Board Room, New York, NY 10004.
### [p050_c0021] Registering for the Public Hearing
[p050_c0022] To register to speak at the hybrid public hearing, please sign up to comment either in-person, online at https://new.mta.info/2025-capital-projects-hearing , or call the Public Hearing Hotline at (646) 252-6777. Registration for the public hearing will open on January 3, 2025. All comments will be transcribed and included as part of the permanent record of this hearing.
[p050_c0023] Joining the Public Hearing If you are registered to speak, you may join the Zoom webinar either online, in-person or by telephone following these instructions:
- [p050_c0024] • Join Zoom Webinar Online: To access the Zoom webinar online, visit the website: https://new.mta.info/2025-capital-projects-hearing . You can also go to zoom.us/join and enter the Webinar (Webinar ID: 812 0352 3827) • Join by PC/Tablet/Smartphone: Use the following link: https://mta.zoom.us/j/81203523827 - (Webinar ID: 812 0352 3827) • Join Zoom Webinar by Telephone: To access the Zoom webinar by telephone, please call 1-877-853-5247 (toll-free). Her enter Webinar ID, Webinar ID: 812 0352 3827, followed by the pound (#) sign. • View Only Online: Members of the public who wish only to view the hearings may access the event live at https://new.mta.info/2025-capital-projects-hearing .
[p050_c0025] To Submit Comments All written comments must be submitted by 6:00 p.m. on February 4, 2025. Comments received after that date and time will not be considered.
[p050_c0026] To View a Copy of the Complete MTA Program Projects A complete list of projects eligible for funding under this proposal is available online and on the MTA Website at https://new.mta.info/2025-capital-projects-hearing
[p050_c0027] Information on public viewing locations and on the projects eligible for funding can be found on the MTA website or by contacting the MTA at the address below. The MTA will also make the final program of projects available on its website https://new.mta.info/transparency/grant-management for Section 5307 funded projects when the FTA awards the Federal Fiscal Year 2025 grants.
### [p050_c0028] Additional Ways to Comment or Request Information
[p050_c0029] For more information, to pre-register to speak, or to submit comments, use the following methods:
- [p050_c0030] • Online: Email comments or register to speak on the MTA website https://new.mta.info/2025-capital-projects-hearing • By Phone: Call the Public Hearing Hotline at (646) 252-6777. Telephone agents are available from 6 a.m. to 10 p.m. daily. • In Person: Submit comments or register during the public hearing.
### [p050_c0031] Guidelines for Verbal and Written Testimony
- [p050_c0032] • Verbal presentations will be limited to two (2) minutes. • Written statements may be submitted as a supplement oral testimony concerning the proposed projects.
### [p050_c0033] Mailing Address for Written Comments:
- [p050_c0034] • To submit comments, for additional information, or to request copies of the application and final program of projects to be submitted to the FTA, letters should be addressed to: MTA Capital Program Funding / Grants Management, RE: 2025 Capital Projects Public Hearing, 2 Broadway, D16.94, New York, NY 10004.
[p050_c0035] All letters must be postmarked by February 4, 2025.
[p050_c0036] Comments received after that date will not be included in the transcript.
### [p050_c0037] Accessibility and Language Assistance Services
[p050_c0038] At the public hearing, CART Captioning and American Sign Language (ASL) services will be available.
[p050_c0039] • For Individuals Who Are Deaf or Hard of Hearing: Use your preferred relay service or the free 711 relay service to connect to the Public Hearing Hotline at (646) 252-6777.
[p050_c0040] • For Individuals Who Are Blind or Have Low Vision: Request accommodations on or before February 1, 2025, either: o Online: https://new.mta.info/2025-capital-projects-hearing , or o By Telephone: Call the Public Hearing Hotline at (646) 252-6777.
[p050_c0041] • For Those Without Internet Access: Dial into the hearing by calling the Zoom webinar at 1-877-853-5247 (toll-free) then enter Webinar ID: 812 0352 3827, followed by the pound (#) sign.
[p050_c0042] • For Language Assistance or Other Accommodations: Submit a request at least three business days before the hearing date in one of the following ways: o Online: https://new.mta.info/2025-capital-projects-hearing o By Phone: Call the Public Hearing Hotline at (646) 252-6777, or
[p050_c0043] By Mail: Send a letter to: MTA Capital Program Funding / Grants Management, RE: 2025 Capital Projects Public Hearing, 2 Broadway, D16.94, New York, NY 10004
[p050_c0044] Metropolitan Transportation Authority
### [p050_c0045] Appendix c: Public Hearing Notice – Capital Projects
[p050_c0046] _46_
[p050_c0047] _Master Page # 50 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 51

[p051_c0001] Project Manager Name Olga Chernat, Deputy Chief, Financial Services
[p051_c0002] Table of Contents Ref #
[p051_c0003]

```text
Board Action- Order To Date App Info Other 1 Finance 02/24/2025 x 2 Board 02/26/2025 x
```
[p051_c0004]

```text
Internal Approvals Order Approval Order Approval 1 Legal 2 Chief of Staff
```
### [p051_c0005] Purpose
[p051_c0006] To secure MTA Board approval to file for and accept Federal grants for Federal Fiscal Year (FFY) 2025.
### [p051_c0007] Discussion
[p051_c0008] Enclosed is a resolution for Board action prepared in conformance with past practices to:
- [p051_c0009] • Authorize the Chairman or any of his designees to make required certifications and assurances. • Authorize the filing and acceptance of grant applications for FFY 2025 Federal appropriations.
[p051_c0010] Projects authorized to be submitted for Federal funding are those referenced in the published Notice of Public Hearing and described in the MTA Description of Projects for FFY 2025. A copy of the resolution requesting MTA Board approval and the list of the projects are attached to this staff summary.
[p051_c0011] In accordance with FTA regulations, a public hearing inviting public comment on the projects proposed to be submitted for federal funding was advertised beginning on January 3, 2025. Written comments (via mail or the MTA.info website) were solicited, and a hearing to solicit comments was held on Tuesday, February 4, 2025 (both in person and via Zoom) (the "Hearing"). At the Hearing, the Director of Capital Funding Management read into the record the amounts of Federal funds and dollar amounts of work covered by the Notice.
[p051_c0012] A transcript of the Hearing and written comments submitted in conjunction with the Hearing are available on the Director's Desk. Sixteen members of the public gave testimony at the Public Hearing, and ten members gave comments via zoom during public hearing. In addition, 23 on-line comments and one written note were collected. Collectively, these statements/comments discussed requests for increased federal funding raised important concerns and suggestions for improving the MTA's operations and future projects. Key points included advocating for more formula funding and enhanced station and train accessibility. Many participants stressed the need for better accessibility signage and support for a rail connection between MTA and NJ Transit's Long Branch Line. There were concerns about projects running over budget and behind schedule, with some suggesting that MTA should not receive additional funding until these issues are addressed. Questions were raised about the cost of elevators in passenger stations, and the need for more elevators per station, especially in Queens, was highlighted. The public also requested alterations to ensure full elevator access at stations and an increase in security measures. There was strong support for the extension of routes such as Interboro to the Bronx, the Queens Link to Rockaway Beach, and the Astoria Line extension to LaGuardia. Other comments included suggestions for vibrant colors in station renovations, support for the SAS Phase 2, and the investment in IBX.
### [p051_c0013] Recommendation
[p051_c0014] It is recommended that the MTA Board approve the attached resolution in order to permit the filing and acceptance of Federal assistance for FFY 2025.

## Page 64

### [p064_c0001] TITLE VI
[p064_c0002] Long Island Rail Road
### [p064_c0003] Civil Rights: Filing a Title VI Complaint with MTA Long Island Rail Road
[p064_c0004] MTA Long Island Rail Road is committed to providing non-discriminatory service to ensure that no person is excluded from participation in, or denied the benefits of, or subjected to discrimination in the receipt of its services on the basis of race, color, or national origin as protected by the Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 ("Title VI"). To request more information about Title VI or to submit a written complaint if you believe that you have been subjected to discrimination, you may contact the Office of Diversity Management, 93-02 Sulphur Blvd., 4th Floor, Jamaica, NY 11435. Phone: 511. Fax: 718-558-7772.
[p064_c0005] In addition to your right to file a complaint with MTA Long Island Rail Road, you have the right to file a Title VI complaint with the U.S. Department of Transportation, Federal Transit Administration, Office of Civil Rights, Attention: Complaint Team, East Building 5th Floor – TOR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p064_c0006] To download a Title VI Complaint Form please visit web.mta.info/tir/about/TitleVI.htm or scan the Title VI Quick Response (QR) code.
### [p064_c0007] Los Derechos Civiles: Póliza para Someter Querellas del Título VI con MTA Long Island Rail Road
[p064_c0008] MTA Long Island Rail Road se compromete a proporcionar un servicio no discriminatorio para garantizar que ninguna persona quede excluida de participar, se le nieguen los beneficios o esté sujeta a discriminación en la recepción de sus servicios por motivos de raza, color o origen nacional según los protegidos por el Título VI de la Ley de Derechos Civiles de 1964 ("Título VI"). Para solicitar más información sobre el Título VI, le invitamos a presentar una queja por escrito si cree que ha sido objeto de discriminación, puede comunicarse con la Oficina de Gestión de la Diversidad, 93-02 Sulphur Blvd., 4th Floor, Jamaica, NY 11435. Teléfono: 511; Fax 718-558-7772.
[p064_c0009] Además de su derecho a someter una queja contra la MTA Long Island Rail Road, también puede someter una queja al Título VI del Ministerio de Transporte de los U.S., a la siguiente dirección: U.S. Department of Transportation, Federal Transit Administration, Office of Civil Rights, Attention: Complaint Team, East Building 5th Floor – TOR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p064_c0010] Para descargar un Formulario de Quejas del Título VI por favor visite web.mta.info/tir/about/TitleVI.htm o escanee el código de respuesta rápida del Título VI (QR).
[p064_c0011] Información en otros lenguajes si está disponible en su web site o su teléfono

[p064_c0013] Información en otros idiomas está disponible en nuestro sitio web o por teléfono

## Page 65

[p065_c0001] Let the Public Know TITLE VI Long Island Rail Road Long Island Rail Road, a public transportation agency of the New York State Thruway Authority, is committed to providing equal access to all passengers. This notice is a public statement of the agency's commitment to equal access and opportunity for all passengers. The agency is committed to providing equal access to all passengers, regardless of race, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, or any other characteristic protected by law. Avviso al público: Política de no discriminación y sobre el Título VI Long Island Rail Road, una agencia pública de transporte y transporte autorizada (TIA), se compromete a proveer un acceso equitativo a todos los pasajeros. Esta declaración es una declaración pública de la agencia de su compromiso a un acceso equitativo y oportunidad para todos los pasajeros, independientemente de su raza, color, origen nacional, orientación sexual, identidad de género, edad, discapacidad, o cualquier otra característica protegida por la ley. Atención: La agencia de transporte pública de Long Island Rail Road, autorizada por la Autoridad del Tránsito, se compromete a proveer un acceso equitativo a todos los pasajeros. Esta declaración es una declaración pública de la agencia de su compromiso a un acceso equitativo y oportunidad para todos los pasajeros, independientemente de su raza, color, origen nacional, orientación sexual, identidad de género, edad, discapacidad, o cualquier otra característica protegida por la ley. PARA MÁS INFORMACIÓN: 800-827-4477 800-827-4477 MTA info 5
[p065_c0002] LIRR Stations and Fare Zones MTA Long Island
[p065_c0003] _Master Page # 65 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 66

### [p066_c0001] TITLE VI
[p066_c0002] Long Island Rail Road
### [p066_c0003] Notice to the Public: Title VI/Nondiscrimination Policy
[p066_c0004] Long Island Rail Road, an affiliated and subsidiary agency of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA), is committed to providing non-discriminatory service to ensure that no person is excluded from participation in, or denied the benefits of, or subjected to discrimination in the receipt of their services on the basis of race, color or national origin as protected by Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (Title VI), and age, sex, disability or religion as protected by other Federal laws. To file a written complaint or request more information about MTA's Title VI and Related Nondiscrimination Laws Policy, visit www.mta.info/transparency/title-vi . To view the Long Island Rail Road Diversity and Equal Opportunity Division's Agency Chief EO Officer, Diversity and Equal Opportunity Division, Jamaica Station, 4th Fl., Jamaica, NY 11435.
[p066_c0005] In addition to your right to file a complaint with Long Island Rail Road, you have the right to file a Title VI complaint with the U.S. Department of Transportation, Federal Transit Administration, Office of Civil Rights, Attention: Complaint Team, East Building 5th Floor - TCR, 2000 Janney Ave SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p066_c0006] To download a Title VI Complaint Form please visit www.mta.info/title-vi or scan the QR code.
[p066_c0007] FOR LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE - CALL 511 or 718-257-5477
### [p066_c0008] Aviso al público: Política de no discriminación y sobre el Título VI
[p066_c0009] Long Island Rail Road, una agencia afiliada y subsidiaria de la Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA), se compromete a prestar un servicio sin discriminación a fin de garantizar que ninguna persona quede excluida de participar, que se le neguen los beneficios, o la prestación de discriminación en la recepción de los servicios por motivos de raza, color o origen de origen, como se estipula en el Título VI de la Ley de Derechos Civiles de 1964 (Título VI), ni por motivos de edad, sexo, discapacidad o religión, conforme las disposiciones que brindan otras leyes federales de derechos civiles (42 USC 2000a). Para presentar una denuncia por escrito o para solicitar más información sobre el Título VI de la MTA y la Política de Leyes de No Discriminación Relacionadas, las personas pueden visitar www.mta.info/transparency/title-vi o acudir en persona en contacto con la División de Diversidad e Igualdad de Oportunidades de Long Island Rail Road en Agency Chief EO Officer, Diversity and Equal Opportunity Division, Jamaica Station, 4th Fl., Jamaica, NY 11435.
[p066_c0010] Además de su derecho a presentar una queja ante Long Island Rail Road, usted tiene derecho a presentar una queja en términos del Título VI ante el U.S. Department of Transportation, Federal Transit Administration, Office of Civil Rights, Attention: Complaint Team, East Building 5th Floor - TCR, 2000 Janney Ave SE, Washington DC 20590.
[p066_c0011] Para descargar un formulario de queja conténgase al Título VI, visite www.mta.info/title-vi o escanee el código QR.
[p066_c0012] PARA REQUISITOS DE ASISTENCIA LINGÜÍSTICA - LLAME AL 511 o 718-257-5477
[p066_c0013] Information in other languages is available on this and other MTA websites. Información en español está disponible en nuestro sitio web y en otras webs. 可以在此网站的其他页面上找到其他语言的MTA网站信息。 Información en otros idiomas está disponible en nuestro sitio web y en otras webs. 其他语言的MTA网站信息可以在其他MTA网站上找到。 Información en otros idiomas está disponible en nuestro sitio web y en otras webs. 其他语言的MTA网站信息可以在其他MTA网站上找到。
[p066_c0014] 511 or 517-690-5114 English New York State
### [p066_c0015] LIRR care
[p066_c0016] Need help getting on or off the train?
### [p066_c0017] Call ahead:
- [p066_c0018] • Available at any station 24/7. • Dial 718-LIRR-CARE (718-547-7227). Then press option 2. • Call at least two hours before your train's departure time. We'll notify the crew to expect you.
### [p066_c0019] Real-time assistance:
- [p066_c0020] • Available at Penn Station, Jamaica, and Atlantic Terminal 6 a.m. to 10 p.m., seven days a week. • Ask an Ambassador, or at the ticket window. • Arrive at least 15 minutes before your train's scheduled departure.
[p066_c0021] www.mta.info/accessibility/lirr-care
[p066_c0022] 718-LIRR-CARE (718-547-7227)
[p066_c0023] _Master Page # 66 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

[p067_c0001] Weekends Brooklyn service Apr 20 - Aug 10 11PM to 4:45 AM No weekend LIRR service between Jamaica and Atlantic Terminal Please take today's service instead Service between Jamaica and Atlantic Terminal is not available on the following dates: April 20, 2025 10:00 AM, 10:30 AM, 11:00 AM, 11:30 AM, 12:00 PM, 12:30 PM, 1:00 PM, 1:30 PM, 2:00 PM, 2:30 PM, 3:00 PM, 3:30 PM, 4:00 PM, 4:30 PM, 5:00 PM, 5:30 PM, 6:00 PM, 6:30 PM, 7:00 PM, 7:30 PM, 8:00 PM, 8:30 PM, 9:00 PM, 9:30 PM, 10:00 PM, 10:30 PM, 11:00 PM, 11:30 PM
[p067_c0002] TITLE VI Long Island Rail Road Notice to the Public: Title VI/Nondiscrimination Policy Long Island Rail Road (LIRR) is committed to providing fair and equal treatment to all passengers and employees. In accordance with Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, as amended, and Executive Order 12958, LIRR does not discriminate on the basis of race or ethnicity in any of its programs or activities. This notice is provided to inform the public of LIRR's commitment to nondiscrimination and to provide information on how to report any form of discrimination or harassment. Avise al público: Política de no discriminacion y sobre el Título VI LIRR se compromete a proporcionar un trato justo y equitativo a todos los pasajeros y empleados. En conformidad con el Título VI de la Ley de Derechos Civiles de 1964, como se modificó, y el Orden Ejecutivo 12958, LIRR no discrimina por razón de raza o etnicidad en ninguno de sus programas o actividades. Este aviso se proporciona para informar al público del compromiso de LIRR con la no discriminación y para proporcionar información sobre cómo reportar cualquier forma de discriminación o acoso. FOR LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE: 800-337-6770 or 718-233-2000 AVISE SOBRE ASISTENCIA EN LENGUA: 800-337-6770 o 718-233-2000 511 877-222-2234 Online text service

## Page 69

### [p069_c0001] Nondiscrimination Policy
[p069_c0002] MTA New York City Transit (“NYC Transit”) and MTA Bus Company are committed to providing non-discriminatory service to ensure that no person is excluded from participation in, or denied the benefits of, or subjected to discrimination in the receipt of their services on the basis of race, color or national origin as protected by Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (“Title VI”), and age, sex, disability or religion as protected by other Federal Transit Laws, (49 U.S.C §5332).
[p069_c0003] To request more information about Title VI or to submit a written complaint if you believe that you have been subjected to discrimination, you may visit www.mta.info or contact NYC Transit’s Office of Equal Employment Opportunity, 130 Livingston Street, 3rd Floor, Brooklyn, NY 11201.
[p069_c0004] In addition to your right to file a complaint with NYC Transit, you have the right to file a Title VI complaint with the U.S. Department of Transportation, Federal Transit Administration, Office of Civil Rights, Attention: Complaint Team, East Building 5th Floor—TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p069_c0005] FOR LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE - CALL 511.
[p069_c0006] SPANISH
### [p069_c0007] Nondiscrimination Policy
[p069_c0008] MTA New York City Transit (“NYC Transit”) y MTA Bus Company se comprometen a prestar un servicio sin discriminación a fin de garantizar que ninguna persona quede excluida de participar, se le nieguen los beneficios o sea objeto de discriminación en la recepción de los servicios por motivo de su raza, color o nación de origen, como se estipula en el Título VI de la Ley de Derechos Civiles de 1964 (“Título VI”), and age, sex, disability or religion as protected by other Federal Transit Laws, (49 U.S.C §5332).
[p069_c0009] Para solicitar más información acerca del Título VI o para presentar una queja por escrito si cree que ha sido objeto de discriminación, puede visitar el sitio www.mta.info o comunicarse con la Oficina de Igualdad de Oportunidades en el Empleo de NYC Transit, ubicada en 130 Livingston Street, 3rd Floor, Brooklyn, NY 11201.
[p069_c0010] Además de su derecho a presentar una queja ante NYC Transit, tiene el derecho a presentar una queja en términos del Título VI ante el Departamento de Transporte de los EE. UU., Administración Federal de Tránsito, Oficina de Derechos Civiles, a la atención de: Complaint Team, East Building 5th Floor—TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p069_c0011] FOR LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE - CALL 511
[p069_c0012] CHINESE
### [p069_c0013] Nondiscrimination Policy
[p069_c0014] MTA New York City Transit (“NYC Transit”) 和 MTA Bus Company 致力于提供非歧视性服务，以确保所有人依据 Civil Rights Act of 1964（1964 年民权法案）Title VI（简称“Title VI”）的规定，能够参与这两个机构提供的服务、享受这些服务所带来的福利，不会因为种族、肤色或民族而受到歧视，and age, sex, disability or religion as protected by other Federal Transit Laws, (49 U.S.C §5332).
[p069_c0015] 如果您认为自己遭受了歧视，要获得关于 Title VI 的更多信息，或提交书面投诉，您可以访问 www.mta.info 或联络 NYC Transit 的均等就业机会办公室，地址是：130 Livingston Street, 3rd Floor, Brooklyn, NY 11201.
[p069_c0016] 除了有权向 NYC Transit 提交投诉之外，您还有权向下列机构提交 Title VI 投诉：美国交通部 (U.S. Department of Transportation)、联邦捷运管理局 (Federal Transit Administration) 和民权办公室 (Office of Civil Rights)，收件地址为：Complaint Team, East Building 5th Floor—TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p069_c0017] FOR LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE - CALL 511.
[p069_c0018] RUSSIAN
### [p069_c0019] Nondiscrimination Policy
[p069_c0020] Транспортная служба MTA New York City Transit (“NYC Transit”) и автобусная компания MTA г. Нью-Йорка обязуются оказывать услуги на недискриминационной основе и гарантируют, что никто не будет лишен права пользования услугами, не получит отказ в предоставлении льгот и не подвергнется дискриминации при пользовании услугами из-за своей расы, цвета кожи или национального происхождения, как указано в Разделе VI Закона о гражданских правах от 1964 года (“Раздел VI”), and age, sex, disability or religion as protected by other Federal Transit Laws, (49 U.S.C §5332).
[p069_c0021] Если вы считаете, что были подвергнуты дискриминации, и хотите получить дополнительную информацию о Разделе VI или подать письменную жалобу, вы можете это сделать на нашем веб-сайте ( www.mta.info ) или обратиться в офис NYC Transit Office of Equal Employment Opportunity (Отдел по равным возможностям трудоустройства), по адресу: 130 Livingston Street, 3-й этаж, Brooklyn, NY 11201.
[p069_c0022] В дополнение к вашему праву подать жалобу в NYC Transit, вы имеете право подать жалобу согласно Разделу VI в Министерство транспорта США, Федеральную администрацию по пассажирским перевозкам, а также в Управление по гражданским правам. Внимание: отдел жалоб находится по адресу: East Building 5th Floor—TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p069_c0023] FOR LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE - CALL 511.
[p069_c0024] KOREAN
### [p069_c0025] Nondiscrimination Policy
[p069_c0026] MTA 뉴욕시 대중교통과 (“뉴욕시 대중교통”) 및 MTA 버스 회사는 1964년에 제정된 민권법 Title VI (“Title VI”)에 의거하여 인종, 피부색 또는 출신 국가로 인해 서비스에서 배제하거나 혜택을 거부하거나 차별을 받지 않도록 보장하는 차별없는 서비스를 제공하기 위해 노력합니다, and age, sex, disability or religion as protected by other Federal Transit Laws, (49 U.S.C §5332).
[p069_c0027] Title VI에 대한 정보를 요청하거나 귀하께서 차별을 경험했다고 판단하여 서면 불만을 제기하려면 www.mta.info 웹사이트를 방문하거나 NYC Transit’s Office of Equal Employment Opportunity (뉴욕시 대중교통과 고용 평등 기회 사무실), 130 Livingston Street, 3rd Floor, Brooklyn, NY 11201으로 문의하십시오.
[p069_c0028] 귀하는 뉴욕시 대중교통국에 불만을 제기할 수 있는 권리 이외에 다음 주소의 미국 교통부 연방 대중교통 관리국 민권 사무소에 Title VI 불만을 제기할 권리가 있습니다: Attention: Complaint Team, East Building 5th Floor—TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p069_c0029] FOR LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE - CALL 511.
[p069_c0030] CREOLE
### [p069_c0031] Nondiscrimination Policy
[p069_c0032] MTA New York City Transit (“NYC Transit”) ak MTA Bus Company angaje pou yo bay sèvis san diskriminasyon pou asire pèsòn moun pa rete san patisiye nan avantaj yo, oswa jwenn refi pou yo, oswa sibi diskriminasyon lè y ap resevwa sèvis yo sou baz ras, koulè, orijin nasyonal jan sa prewa nan Tit VI Lwa sou Dwa Sivil 1964 la (“Title VI”), and age, sex, disability or religion as protected by other Federal Transit Laws, (49 U.S.C §5332).
[p069_c0033] Pou mande plis enfòmasyon sou Tit VI oswa pou depoze yon plent alekri si w panse ou te sibi diskriminasyon, ou ka ale sou www.mta.info oswa kontakte NYC Transit’s Office of Equal Employment Opportunity, 130 Livingston Street, 3rd Floor, Brooklyn, NY 11201.
[p069_c0034] Anplis de dwa ou genyen pou depoze yon plent nan NYC Transit, ou gen dwa pou depoze yon plent Tit VI nan U.S. Department of Transportation, Federal Transit Administration, Office of Civil Rights, Atansyon: Complaint Team, East Building 5th Floor—TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p069_c0035] FOR LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE - CALL 511.
[p069_c0036] BENGALI
### [p069_c0037] Nondiscrimination Policy
[p069_c0038] MTA New York City Transit (“NYC Transit”) এবং MTA Bus Company 1964 সালের (“Title VI”) নাগরিক অধিকার আইনের Title VI-এর দ্বারা সুরক্ষিত জাতি, বর্ণ বা জাতীয় উভয়ের ভিত্তিতে যাতে করে কোনো ব্যক্তি অংশগ্রহণ করার (থেকে বঞ্চিত না হয়, অথবা তাকে সুযোগসুবিধাগুলো থেকে বঞ্চিত করা না হয় অথবা পরিষেবাগুলো প্রাপ্ত করার ক্ষেত্রে তাদের যাতে বৈষম্যের শিকার হতে না হয় তা নিশ্চিত করতে বৈষম্যহীন পরিষেবা সরবরাহ করার জন্য প্রতিশ্রুতিবদ্ধ। and age, sex, disability or religion as protected by other Federal Transit Laws, (49 U.S.C §5332).
[p069_c0039] Title VI-এর সম্পর্কে আরও তথ্যের জন্য অথবা আপনার যদি মনে হয় যে আপনাকে বৈষম্যের শিকার হতে হচ্ছে, তাহলে লিখিত অভিযোগ জমা দেওয়ার জন্য আপনি www.mta.info -তে গিয়ে দেখতে পারেন অথবা NYC Transit’s Office of Equal Employment Opportunity (NYC Transit এর সমান কর্মসংস্থান সুযোগের অফিসে) যোগাযোগ করতে পারেন, 130 Livingston Street, 3rd Floor, Brooklyn, NY 11201।
[p069_c0040] আপনার কাছে NYC Transit-এ অভিযোগ দায়ের করার অধিকারের পাশাপাশি, U.S. Department of Transportation (মার্কিন যুক্তরাষ্ট্রের পরিবহণ অধিদফতর), Federal Transit Administration (ফেডারেল ট্রানজিট প্রশাসন), Office of Civil Rights (নাগরিক অধিকার অফিস)-এ, আপনার কাছে একটি Title VI-এর বিষয়ে অভিযোগ করার অধিকার রয়েছে, মনোযোগ দিন: Complaint Team, East Building 5th Floor—TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p069_c0041] FOR LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE - CALL 511.
[p069_c0042] _Master Page # 69 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 71

### [p071_c0001] Nondiscrimination Policy
[p071_c0002] MTA New York City Transit ("NYC Transit") and MTA Bus Company are committed to providing non-discriminatory service to ensure that no person is excluded from participation in, or denied the benefits of, or subjected to discrimination in the receipt of their services on the basis of race, color or national origin as protected by Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 ("Title VI"), and age, sex, disability or religion as protected by other Federal Transit Laws. (49 U.S.C §5332).
[p071_c0003] To request more information about Title VI or to submit a written complaint if you believe that you have been subjected to discrimination, you may visit www.mta.info or contact NYC Transit's Office of Equal Employment Opportunity, 130 Livingston Street, 3rd Floor, Brooklyn, NY 11201.
[p071_c0004] In addition to your right to file a complaint with NYC Transit, you have the right to file a Title VI complaint with the U.S. Department of Transportation, Federal Transit Administration, Office of Civil Rights, Attention: Complaint Team, East Building 5th Floor—TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p071_c0005] FOR LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE - CALL 511.
[p071_c0006] SPANISH
### [p071_c0007] Nondiscrimination Policy
[p071_c0008] MTA New York City Transit ("NYC Transit") y MTA Bus Company se comprometen a prestar un servicio sin discriminación a fin de garantizar que ninguna persona quede excluida de participar, o le neguen los beneficios a ser objeto de discriminación en la recepción de los servicios en motivo de su raza, color o nacionalidad de origen, como se estipula en el Título VI de la Ley de Derechos Civiles de 1964 ("Título VI"), edad, sexo, discapacidad o religión como protegidos por otras Leyes Federales de Transitos. (49 U.S.C. §5332).
[p071_c0009] Para solicitar más información acerca del Título VI o para presentar una queja por escrito si cree que ha sido objeto de discriminación, puede visitar el sitio www.mta.info o comunicarse con la Oficina de Igualdad de Oportunidades en el Empleo de NYC Transit, ubicada en 130 Livingston Street, 3rd Floor, Brooklyn, NY 11201.
[p071_c0010] Además de su derecho a presentar una queja ante NYC Transit, tiene derecho a presentar una queja en términos del Título VI ante el Departamento de Transporte de los EE.UU., Administración Federal de Transitos, Oficina de Derechos Civiles, a la atención de Complaint Team, East Building 5th Floor—TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p071_c0011] FOR LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE - CALL 511.
[p071_c0012] CHINESE
### [p071_c0013] Nondiscrimination Policy
[p071_c0014] MTA New York City Transit ("NYC Transit") 和 MTA Bus Company 致力于提供非歧视性服务，以确保没有人被排除在参与之外，或拒绝其利益，或受到歧视在接收其服务的基础上，种族、肤色或国籍的起源，如规定在 1964 年《民权法案》（“标题 VI”）中规定，年龄、性别、残疾或宗教作为受到其他联邦交通法保护。 (49 U.S.C. §5332).
[p071_c0015] 如果您认为自己遭受了歧视，请获得关于 Title VI 的更多信息，或提交书面投诉，您可以访问 www.mta.info 或联系 NYC Transit 的平等就业机会办公室，地址是：130 Livingston Street, 3rd Floor, Brooklyn, NY 11201.
[p071_c0016] 除了有权向 NYC Transit 提交投诉之外，您还有权向下列机构提交 Title VI 投诉：美国交通部 (U.S. Department of Transportation)、联邦交通管理局 (Federal Transit Administration) 和民权办公室 (Office of Civil Rights)，或转址为 Complaint Team, East Building 5th Floor—TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p071_c0017] FOR LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE - CALL 511.
[p071_c0018] RUSSIAN
### [p071_c0019] Nondiscrimination Policy
[p071_c0020] Транспортная служба MTA New York City Transit ("NYC Transit") и автобусная компания MTA Bus Company обязуются оказывать услуги на недискриминационной основе, гарантируя, что никто не будет лишён права пользоваться услугами, не получив от них в предоставлении услуг и не подвергнувшись дискриминации при предоставлении услуг на своей расе, цвета кожи или национальной принадлежности, как указано в Разделе VI Закона о гражданских правах от 1964 года ("Раздел VI"), и age, sex, disability or religion as protected by other Federal Transit laws. (49 U.S.C. §5332).
[p071_c0021] Если вы считаете, что были подвергнуты дискриминации, и хотите получить дополнительную информацию о Разделе VI или подать письменную жалобу, вы можете это сделать на нашем веб-сайте ( www.mta.info ) или обратиться в офис NYC Transit Office of Equal Employment Opportunity (Отдел по равным возможностям трудоустройства) по адресу: 130 Livingston Street, 3rd этаж, Brooklyn, NY 11201.
[p071_c0022] В дополнение к желанию подать жалобу в NYC Transit, вы имеете право подать жалобу согласно Разделу VIа Министерство транспорта США. Федеральным административным на транспортном, а также в Управление по гражданским правам. Внутренний отдел жалоб находится по адресу: East Building 5th Floor—TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p071_c0023] FOR LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE - CALL 511.
[p071_c0024] KOREAN
### [p071_c0025] Nondiscrimination Policy
[p071_c0026] MTA 뉴욕시 대중교통과 뉴욕시 대중교통버스 및 MTA 버스는 1964년에 제정된 민권법 Title VI의 제 6 조에 따라 모든 고객에게 동등한 서비스를 제공하고 서비스에서 차별하거나 배제하거나 차별적 반격 없도록 보장하는 차별 없는 서비스를 제공하기 위해 노력합니다. and age, sex, disability or religion as protected by other Federal Transit Laws. (49 U.S.C. §5332).
[p071_c0027] Title VI에 대한 정보를 요청하거나 차별에서 차별을 줄입니다.라고 말하면서 서면 문건을 제기하려면 www.mta.info 웹사이트를 방문하거나 NYC Transit's Office of Equal Employment Opportunity(뉴욕시 대중교통평등과 고용 평등, 기회 잡부실), 130 Livingston Street, 3rd Floor, Brooklyn, NY 11201의 로 방문하십시오.
[p071_c0028] 구체적 차별에 대한 구체적인 문제를 제기하고 있는 경우 이외의 다른 주소에 미국 교통법 연방 대중교통 관련국 민간 사무소에 Title VI 불만을 제기할 권리가 있습니다. Attention: Complaint Team East Building 5th Floor—TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.

### [p071_c0031] Nondiscrimination Policy
[p071_c0032] MTA New York City Transit ("NYC Transit") and MTA Bus Company agree to ensure that no person is excluded from participation in, or denied the benefits of, or subjected to discrimination in the receipt of their services on the basis of race, color or national origin as protected by Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 ("Title VI"), and age, sex, disability or religion as protected by other Federal Transit Laws. (49 U.S.C. §5332).
[p071_c0033] You may file a complaint against NYC Transit or report your problem directly to the office of the Office of Equal Employment Opportunity, 130 Livingston Street, 3rd Floor, Brooklyn, NY 11201.
[p071_c0034] Aside from your right to report your problem to NYC Transit, you also have the right to file a Title VI complaint with the U.S. Department of Transportation, Federal Transit Administration, Office of Civil Rights, Attention: Complaint Team, East Building 5th Floor—TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave. SE, Washington, DC 20590.
[p071_c0035] FOR LANGUAGE ASSISTANCE - CALL 511.

[p071_c0038] MTA New York City Transit ("NYC Transit") and MTA Bus Company agree to ensure that no person is excluded from participation in, or denied the benefits of, or subjected to discrimination in the receipt of their services on the basis of race, color or national origin as protected by Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 ("Title VI"), and age, sex, disability or religion as protected by other Federal Transit Laws. (49 U.S.C. §5332).
[p071_c0039] Title VI에 대한 정보를 요청하거나 차별에서 차별을 줄입니다.라고 말하면서 서면 문건을 제기하려면 www.mta.info 웹사이트를 방문하거나 NYC Transit's Office of Equal Employment Opportunity(뉴욕시 대중교통평등과 고용 평등, 기회 잡부실), 130 Livingston Street, 3rd Floor, Brooklyn, NY 11201의 로 방문하십시오.

### [p072_c0002] MTA -WIDE TITLE VI PROGRAM UPDATE
[p072_c0003] July 1, 2022- June 30, 2025
### [p072_c0004] Exhibit 3
### [p072_c0005] Title VI and Related Nondiscrimination Laws Complaint Management
- [p072_c0006] • All-Agency Policy Directive • Title VI Complaint Form
