## Page 444

### [p444_c0001] Exhibit 11: Performance Monitoring Metro-North Railroad
### [p444_c0002] Metro-North Railroad Title VI Program Update July 1, 2022 – June 30, 2025
### [p444_c0003] Exhibit 11: Performance Monitoring
### [p444_c0004] Part I: Introduction, Test Method, and Summary of Results
[p444_c0005] Data Source:
[p444_c0006] Minority Data: Census 2020.
[p444_c0007] Income Data: ACS 2018-2022 5-Year Estimates.
[p444_c0008] Station Population: Total population of Census Tracts within 2.5 miles of each station.
[p444_c0009] _Master Page # 444 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 445

### [p445_c0001] Exhibit 11: Performance Monitoring Metro-North Railroad
### [p445_c0002] Introduction
[p445_c0003] Metro-North Railroad monitors the performance of our transit system based on the Board-approved system-wide service standards and service policies and reports the monitoring results to the MTA's Department of Diversity and Civil Rights (DDCR) on an annual basis. A three-year Title VI report is also prepared for inclusion in the MTA-wide submission to the FTA.
[p445_c0004] As part of the service equity analysis, Metro-North Railroad uses quantitative measures of testing: t-Test and Chi-square statistical tests, to ascertain if there are significant differences in the service provided to minority vs. non-minority and/or low-income vs. non-low-income population. Use of either of these two statistical tests is dependent on the type of data being analyzed.
[p445_c0005] Metro-North monitoring assessment was completed for each of the board-approved standards and policies to determine whether disparate impacts exist for the minority and low-income populations. These assessments are detailed, along with the monitoring results, in this Exhibit.
[p445_c0006] Service Monitoring test results show no significant differences except for the Minority analysis for headway for the Hudson Line Peak Service, as well as the result restroom allocations in the low-income stations. Headway at the Minority stations can be mitigated by the assignment of more trains for the Peak service. Regarding the allocation of restrooms, some minority and low-income stations are located in heavily built-up environments, often without station houses to accommodate restrooms.
### [p445_c0007] Methodology
### [p445_c0008] Census Analysis
[p445_c0009] As part of Service Monitoring, each Metro-North station and train is classified as minority / non-minority and low-income / non-low-income based on the station location and the census tract population within 2.5 mile buffer of the station. Non-White population from Census 2020 is categorized as minority population and the population below poverty in the S1701 table from ACS 2018-2022 5-Year Estimates is categorized as low-income population.
### [p445_c0010] Classification of minority and income status of Metro-North stations
[p445_c0011] For the classification of Metro-North station as minority / non-minority and low-income / non-low-income, each station is identified as a "city" station if it is within New York City (in the Bronx or New York counties) and "suburban" if it is located outside of the Bronx or New York counties.
[p445_c0012] A minority threshold and a low-income threshold is calculated separately for the city and suburban stations. The minority threshold for city / suburban station is the average percentage of minority population served by the corresponding city/suburban stations, while the low-income threshold is the average percentage of low-income population served by the same stations.
[p445_c0013] If the percentage of minority / low-income population served by a station is above the corresponding minority / low-income threshold, then the station is classified as a minority / low-income station. If the percentage of minority / low-income population served by a
[p445_c0014] Data Source:
[p445_c0015] Minority Data: Census 2020.
[p445_c0016] Income Data: ACS 2018-2022 5-Year Estimates.
[p445_c0017] Station Population: Total population of Census Tracts within 2.5 miles of each station.
[p445_c0018] _Master Page # 445 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 446

### [p446_c0001] Exhibit 11: Performance Monitoring Metro-North Railroad
[p446_c0002] station is below the corresponding minority / low-income threshold, then the station is classified as a non-minority / non-low-income station.
### [p446_c0003] Classification of minority and income status of Metro-North trains
[p446_c0004] Each Metro-North train is classified as a minority / non-minority and a low-income / non-low-income train based on the aggregate population of the stations the train stops at. If the percentage of minority / low-income population served by the train is above the threshold or the average percentage of minority / low-income population served by all trains, then the train is classified as minority / low-income. If the percentage of minority / low-income population served by the train is below the threshold, then the train is classified as non-minority / non-low-income.
### [p446_c0005] Test Method
[p446_c0006] Metro-North uses the following techniques to monitor the performance of the transit system relative to system-wide service standards and policies:
- [p446_c0007] * On-Time Performance: A t-Test statistical analysis is performed by line to test whether the trains primarily serving customers in minority and low-income areas received disparate OTP during peak and off-peak service. * Vehicle Assignment: A t-Test statistical analysis is performed by line to test whether the trains primarily serving customers in minority and low-income areas received statistically significant disparate vehicle assignments with regard to fleet age during peak and off-peak service. * Vehicle Load: A t-Test statistical analysis is performed by line to test whether the trains primarily serving customers in the minority and low-income areas had higher vehicle occupancy during peak and off-peak service. * Vehicle Headway: A t-Test statistical analysis is performed to measure if there were disparities in the frequency of service to the minority and low-income stations during peak and off-peak service. * Service Availability: A t-Test statistical analysis is performed by line to measure whether there were disparities on the availability of service measured in terms of distance to the station in the minority and low-income areas. * Transit Amenities: A Chi-Square statistical analysis is performed to determine if there are any disparities in the distribution of station amenities to the minority and low-income stations.
[p446_c0008] Data Source:
[p446_c0009] Minority Data: Census 2020.
[p446_c0010] Income Data: ACS 2018-2022 5-Year Estimates.
[p446_c0011] Station Population: Total population of Census Tracts within 2.5 miles of each station.
[p446_c0012] _Master Page # 446 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 447

### [p447_c0001] Exhibit 11: Performance Monitoring Metro-North Railroad
### [p447_c0002] Summary of Results - Service Standards and Policies
[p447_c0003]

```text
Service Standards and Policies Service Measure Standard Analysis Results – Minority Analysis Results – Poverty Analysis Notes/Mitigation On-time Performance Measure of trains arriving at their final destination within 5:59 of their scheduled arrival time. No early departures. A statistical analysis is performed to determine whether there are any disparities in the OTP of minority vs. non-minority and low-income vs. non-low-income trains on Metro-North’s Hudson, Harlem, and New Haven Lines. t-Test No significant difference No significant difference Vehicle Assignment Assignment of vehicles based on operating characteristics, capacity, and vehicle availability. A statistical analysis is performed to determine if there are any disparities in the age of the vehicle assigned to minority vs. non-minority, and low-income vs. non-low-income trains on Metro-North’s Hudson, Harlem, and New Haven Lines. t-Test No significant difference No significant difference Vehicle Load Metro-North Service Standards recommends lengthening trains if occupancy exceeds 95% for the Peak and 85% for the Off-Peak trains. Trains may be lengthened to provide additional seating, and are subject to equipment availability, and platform lengths. A statistical analysis is performed to determine if there is any difference between load factors on minority vs. non-minority, and low-income vs. non low-income Metro-North’s trains. t-Test No significant difference No significant difference (With the exception of income analyses on New Haven Line for Peak and Off-Peak service). The income analyses result on the New Haven Line can be attributed to assignment of 99% of the trains as Non-Low-Income.
```
[p447_c0004] Data Source:
[p447_c0005] Minority Data: Census 2020.
[p447_c0006] Income Data: ACS 2018-2022 5-Year Estimates.
[p447_c0007] Station Population: Total population of Census Tracts within 2.5 miles of each station.
[p447_c0008] _Master Page # 447 of 1629 - Diversity Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 448

### [p448_c0001] Exhibit 11: Performance Monitoring Metro-North Railroad
[p448_c0002]

```text
Service Standards and Policies Service Measure Standard Analysis Results – Minority Analysis Results – Poverty Analysis Notes/Mitigation Vehicle Headway Maximum vehicle headway varies by operating line segment and time of day for Metro-North stations. A statistical analysis is performed to determine if there is any difference between average headways for minority vs. non-minority, and low-income vs. non-low-income stations on Metro-North’s Hudson, Harlem, and New Haven Lines. t-Test No significant difference No significant difference (With the exception of Minority analysis Hudson Line for Peak service). This can be attributed to availability of alternate services in the minority areas. For mitigation, more trains need to be assigned to minority stations. Service Availability The average distance between Metro-North station and the centroids of census tracts within a 2.5-mile buffer area around each station. A statistical analysis is performed to determine if there is any difference in the distance to Metro-North stations from minority vs. non-minority census tracts, and low-income vs. non-low-income census tracts within 2.5 miles of the station. t-Test No significant difference No significant difference
```
[p448_c0003] Data Source:
[p448_c0004] Minority Data: Census 2020.
[p448_c0005] Income Data: ACS 2018-2022 5-Year Estimates.

## Page 456

### [p456_c0001] Exhibit 11: Performance Monitoring Metro-North Railroad
### [p456_c0002] A-1-a. Peak/Off-Peak On Time Performance of Metro-North Trains – Minority Analysis by Line
[p456_c0003]

```text
Metro-North Title VI t-Test: Peak On Time Performance – Minority Analysis Hudson Line Harlem Line New Haven Line Demographic Average Variance Average Variance Average Variance Minority 97.82% 0.00 95.62% 0.00 96.91% 0.00 Non-Minority 97.58% 0.00 96.50% 0.00 97.05% 0.00 t-Statistic -0.29 -0.99 -0.24 t-Critical 2.16 2.20 2.13 Significance Favors Not Significant Not Significant Not Significant Pass or Fail Pass Pass Pass
```
[p456_c0004] **Using the statistical t-Test, there is disparity in favor of minority population.
[p456_c0005]

```text
Metro-North Title VI t-Test: Off-Peak On Time Performance – Minority Analysis Hudson Line Harlem Line New Haven Line Demographic Average Variance Average Variance Average Variance Minority 99.47% 6.54 97.45% 0.00 98.77% 4.83 Non-Minority 97.65% 0.00 97.78% 0.00 97.28% 0.00 t-Statistic 3.96 -0.51 2.12 t-Critical 2.12 2.11 2.09 Significance Favors Minority Persons** Not Significant Minority Persons** Pass or Fail Pass Pass Pass
```
[p456_c0006] **Using the statistical t-Test, there is disparity in favor of minority population.
### [p456_c0007] A-1-b. Peak/Off-Peak On Time Performance of Metro-North Trains – Income Analysis by Line
[p456_c0008]

```text
Metro-North Title VI t-Test: Peak On Time Performance – Income Analysis Hudson Line Harlem Line New Haven Line Demographic Average Variance Average Variance Low-Income 97.82% 0.00 95.53% 0.00 N/A* Non-Low-Income 97.58% 0.00 96.44% 0.00 t-Statistic 0.29 -0.92 t-Critical 2.16 2.26 Significance Favors Not Significant Not Significant Pass or Fail Pass Pass
```
[p456_c0009] *No peak non-low-income status train on New Haven Line.
[p456_c0010] **Using the statistical t-Test, there is disparity in favor of low-income population.
[p456_c0011]

```text
Metro-North Title VI t-Test: Off-Peak On Time Performance – Income Analysis Hudson Line Harlem Line New Haven Line Demographic Average Variance Average Variance Low-Income 99.40% 8.18 97.45% 0.00 N/A* Non-Low-Income 97.40% 0.00 97.95% 0.00 t-Statistic 2.74 -0.84 t-Critical 2.12 2.13 Significance Favors Low-Income Persons** Not Significant Pass or Fail Pass Pass
```
[p456_c0012] *No off-peak non-low-income status train on New Haven Line.
[p456_c0013] **Using the statistical t-Test, there is disparity in favor of low-income population.

### [p463_c0001] Exhibit 11: Performance Monitoring Metro-North Railroad
### [p463_c0002] D-1. Metro-North Stations - Minority Analysis by Line – Hudson Line
